channel.title: Philosophy on Amitav Krishna
channel.description: Recent content in Philosophy on Amitav Krishna

item.title: Why is some philosophy so math-ish?
item.description: Hello! Quick throwaway post here. I was reading Normative Uncertainty by William MacAskill(Effective Altruism man) when I was flashbanged by the math. After some chatting with claude, apparently this is what makes analytic philosophy notable, as opposed to continental philosophy which is focused on society, culture, etc. What they don’t learn about apparently is analysis, which unlike logic, deals with continuous things.
